logs.title: Phase 02 COLLAPSED — App Kit is fully client-side (architecture finding)
logs.what: Phase 02 step 0 spike (cook skill, code mode) ran probes against installed @circle-fin/app-kit v1.6.1. The adapter is the customer's wallet (EIP1193Provider) — cannot be serialized server-side. Therefore the PRD-implied server-side quote/execute dual-path is architecturally impossible. Edited phase-03-payment-page-dual-path.md to declare absorbed sub-tasks (client-side quote, client-side execute, status PATCH endpoint, kit.on wildcard wiring). logs.title: ADR: Drop @circle-fin/app-kit SDK, pivot v2 to direct Arc bridge contract calls
logs.what: Phase 03 step 0 spike (cook skill code mode) ran 3 runtime probes against installed @circle-fin/app-kit v1.6.1: (TEST A) estimateSwap with adapter, no kitKey -> KitError: 'Kit key is required. Expected format: KIT_KEY:<keyId>:<keySecret>'; (TEST B) estimateBridge same -> BigInt serialization error (downstream of auth, also requires kitKey); (TEST C) confirmed Zod schema marking config.kitKey optional is misleading -- runtime enforces required. Confirmed kit key format encodes secret (keyId:keySecret); cannot ship to client bundles via NEXT_PUBLIC_*. Combined with Phase 02 finding (estimate* requires from.adapter = customer wallet, client-only), SDK is structurally incompatible with BYO-wallet payment flows -- needs both customer wallet AND server secret in single call, impossible to satisfy. User picked Option A: drop SDK, call Arc's kitContracts.bridge (0xC5567a5E3370d4DBfB0540025078e283e36A363d) directly via viem writeContract; server reuses existing v1 cctp.ts attestation poll loop, retargeted to Arc CCTP v2 domain 26 (not 7 -- v1 had wrong domain, was dead code in ADMIN_RELAY mode). Wrote plans/reports/adr-260525-2333-drop-app-kit-sdk-use-bridge-contract-directly.md (full context, 3 findings, decision rationale, consequences, alternatives B/C/D rejected, reversibility note, 3 open questions). Updated plan.md with 'Architecture pivot 25/05' callout + ADR link + revised Phase 03-06 descriptions. Rewrote phase-03-payment-page-dual-path.md from scratch as 'Arc bridge contract via writeContract' (step 0 research = Arc bridge ABI from @circle-fin/bridge-kit or explorer, source-chain bridge deployment consistency check, env vars NEXT_PUBLIC_BRIDGE_BACKEND + NEXT_PUBLIC_ARC_BRIDGE_CONTRACT_ADDRESS, page.tsx dual branch, cctp.ts parameterization for arbitrary chain, smoke gate dual-flag local test). No code changes -- Phase 03 implementation defers to next session.
logs.why: Cook skill code-mode review gate caught the SECOND structural blocker in two phases. SDK was the wrong abstraction; PRD framing ('rebuild on Arc App Kit') over-indexed on Circle's developer marketing. Real Arc commerce primitive is the bridge contract (Circle-maintained, well-tested); the SDK is for dev-owned wallets (treasury, Modular Wallets) not BYO-wallet flows. Direct contract pattern: closest to v1, reuses existing cctp.ts polling, eliminates dual-trust-domain auth problem, deletes our custom SwapRouter risk by using Circle's bridge instead.

logs.title: Phase 03 code shipped — v2 CCTP direct from Ethereum Sepolia (strangler-fig)
logs.what: Phase 03 step 0 spike found: bridge-kit doesn't expose kitContracts.bridge ABI; underlying mechanism is standard CCTPv2 (TokenMessenger.depositForBurn + Circle attestation + MessageTransmitter.receiveMessage). BSC Testnet isn't a CCTPv2 chain in the SDK catalog (Algorand, Aptos, Arc, Arbitrum, Avalanche, Base, Celo, Codex, Edge, Ethereum, HyperEVM, Ink, Linea, Optimism, Polygon, ... -- no BSC). v1 ran in ADMIN_RELAY because CCTP was dead code on BSC. User picked Ethereum Sepolia as v2 source chain + standard 2-sig flow (approve + depositForBurn). Implementation: (1) Appended 6 env vars to frontend/.env.local: NEXT_PUBLIC_BRIDGE_BACKEND=v1 (default, strangler-fig safe), NEXT_PUBLIC_SOURCE_CHAIN_ID=11155111, NEXT_PUBLIC_USDC_ADDRESS_SOURCE=0x1c7D4B196Cb..., NEXT_PUBLIC_CCTP_TOKEN_MESSENGER_SOURCE=0x8fe6b999..., NEXT_PUBLIC_ARC_CCTP_DOMAIN=26 (fixes v1 bug where ARC_CCTP_DOMAIN_ID was 7 -- Polygon's domain), ETHEREUM_SEPOLIA_RPC_URL=publicnode. Added missing CCTP_MESSAGE_TRANSMITTER_ARC=0xE737e5cE...CE275 (was unset in local env, only on Vercel). (2) New frontend/lib/cctp-abi.ts (53 lines): minimal ERC20 approve + view ABIs and CCTP TokenMessenger depositForBurn ABI. (3) New frontend/lib/payment-flow-v2.ts (124 lines): usePaymentFlowV2 hook does chain-switch to Sepolia, USDC.approve(TokenMessenger), TokenMessenger.depositForBurn(amount, 26, merchantBytes32, USDC), POSTs burn tx to /api/agent/execute, reads SSE stream. Reuses existing v1 SSE event names (swap_executing, bridging, confirmed) so PaymentProgressBar component is unchanged. (4) frontend/lib/cctp.ts parameterized: added SourceChainConfig interface + V1_BSC_SOURCE/V2_ETH_SEPOLIA_SOURCE convenience builders; extractMessageBytesFromReceipt/extractMessageHash/extractRawMessage now take optional source param (defaults to V1_BSC_SOURCE so existing callers don't break). (5) frontend/app/api/agent/execute/route.ts: top-level BRIDGE_BACKEND env check; v2 branch uses V2_ETH_SEPOLIA_SOURCE for extract calls, emits SSE events with source:'eth-sepolia' metadata, falls through to shared awardPoints; v1 branch wraps existing CCTP/ADMIN_RELAY logic unchanged. (6) frontend/app/pay/[id]/page.tsx: PaymentPageWrapper dispatches to PaymentPage (v1) or new inline PaymentPageV2 (v2) based on NEXT_PUBLIC_BRIDGE_BACKEND. PaymentPageV2 has its own UI (no FeeBreakdownCard since no swap math; simple 'You pay X USDC / Merchant receives X USDC' card + Pay button). Total page.tsx = 348 lines (over 200-line guideline -- strangler-fig transition exception, cleaned up in Phase 06). Build verification: next build 1.65s Turbopack, all 9 routes registered including untouched /api/agent/{quote,execute}, /api/dashboard, /api/dev/appkit-ping. NEXT_PUBLIC_BRIDGE_BACKEND defaults to v1 so production behavior unchanged until Phase 04 flip.

logs.title: Pin chainId on v2 writeContract calls (Sepolia approve+burn)
logs.what: User wallet test surfaced 'Insufficient funds - not enough SepoliaETH for network fees' on approve USDC tx, despite wallet holding 0.716 ETH. Diagnosis ruled out value=non-zero (default 0n, confirmed by reading payment-flow-v2.ts), wrong contract addresses (cast call verified USDC symbol/decimals + TokenMessenger bytecode + approve simulation returning true), and amount-encoding bugs. Real cause: writeContractAsync called without chainId, so wagmi v3 may estimate gas through the default chain transport (first in chains[] = bscTestnet) even though the connector is on Sepolia. Bogus gas estimate against the wrong chain → garbage MetaMask error string. Fix: pin chainId: SOURCE_CHAIN_ID on both writeContractAsync calls in frontend/lib/payment-flow-v2.ts. Build green.
logs.why: Wagmi v3 with multiple registered chains needs explicit chainId to disambiguate transport for estimation. Without it, behavior depends on connector state vs chains[] order.
logs.concerns: Defensive against this class of bug for any future wagmi cross-chain writeContract call: always pin chainId. Could add an ESLint rule or wrapper to enforce, but YAGNI for now — only two such call sites.

logs.title: Enable CCTPv2 Fast Transfer + guard SSE stream crash (v2 bridge step was hanging)
logs.what: User got past approve+burn via OKX wallet (MetaMask Sepolia gas oracle was the earlier blocker -- false insufficient-funds; switching wallet bypassed it). Bridge step then hung, never reached confirmed. Root cause from dev log: POST /api/agent/execute ran 2.2min then unhandledRejection TypeError Invalid state: WritableStream is closed at emit (route.ts:60). Diagnosis: maxFee=0n made Circle treat burn as Standard transfer (waits Sepolia hard finality ~13-19min) while server pollAttestation timeout was only 120s. Verified via Circle Iris sandbox: burn tx 0x43182ad74e9297894e1fdce8d7ecc7ad2fce41c33c18080b7ad9c6866b97f081 status=pending_confirmations, cctpVersion=2. Fee API /v2/burn/USDC/fees/0/26 returned fast(threshold 1000)=1bps min, standard(2000)=0; fast allowance ~100k USDC available, so Fast is supported Sepolia->Arc. Fixes: (1) frontend/lib/payment-flow-v2.ts: maxFee = amountWei/1000n || 1n (0.1% ceiling, >0 to qualify Fast; actual charge ~1bps), removed MAX_FEE=0n const, kept minFinalityThreshold=1000 (now correctly Fast), corrected misleading comments that called 1000 Standard. (2) frontend/app/api/agent/execute/route.ts: wrapped emit() write and writer.close() in try/catch to stop ERR_INVALID_STATE crash on client SSE disconnect; bumped v2 pollAttestation timeout 120s->180s. tsc --noEmit green.

(2) Fast Transfer deducts ~1bps fee at mint so merchant receives amount minus tiny fee -- slightly breaks README exact-USDC promise; gross-up (burn amount+fee) is a future refinement. (3) Server receiveMessage mint on Arc still UNVERIFIED e2e -- needs AIG admin wallet funded with Arc gas; watch this step on next test. (4) On Vercel, holding SSE open up to 180s may exceed serverless function timeout on some plans -- fine locally, verify on prod before Phase 04 flip.

logs.title: v2 CCTP Sepolia->Arc end-to-end WORKING (first successful e2e payment)
logs.what: User reported approve+burn OK but bridge step failed with network error. Root cause: cctp.ts pollAttestation() uses CCTPv1 API pattern (GET {apiBase}/{messageHash}) but our v2 burns need CCTPv2 Iris endpoint (GET /v2/messages/{sourceDomain}?transactionHash={tx}). v1 polling on v2 burns 404s forever -> times out at 180s even though Circle had attestation ready (verified status=complete via direct Iris query on burn 0x9a620cf2ff42df5882a8b424094f4d26dd51bbfdd87d3a2070b34aae4edffa16). Fix: (1) frontend/lib/cctp.ts: new pollAttestationV2(txHash, sourceDomain, timeoutMs) helper -> queries Iris v2, returns {message, attestation}; default base https://iris-api-sandbox.circle.com/v2 overridable via CIRCLE_IRIS_API_V2 env. (2) frontend/app/api/agent/execute/route.ts v2 branch: replaced extractMessageHash/extractRawMessage/pollAttestation chain with single pollAttestationV2 call (Iris v2 returns BOTH raw msg + attestation so on-chain extract step is unnecessary for v2); removed unused V2_ETH_SEPOLIA_SOURCE/SourceChainConfig imports. tsc green. Verified end-to-end: POSTed /api/agent/execute against existing burn 0x9a620... -> SSE emitted swap_executing, bridging, confirmed in seconds. Arc mint tx 0xc0b4cca98ca37963c92d43afaada02c65e093567e6f374d04e667acdf641eb61 status=success at block 44698950, gas 175814. Merchant 0x0809...ceF8 USDC balance on Arc now 40.996383. First time the v2 pipeline has completed end-to-end (Sepolia burn -> Circle attestation -> Arc mint via admin wallet).
logs.why: v2 bridge step kept timing out at 180s despite Circle attestation being ready -- mismatched API version (v1 endpoint vs v2 burn) was the silent killer.
logs.concerns: (1) Burn 0x43182ad7... (the very first Standard test burn, maxFee=0) was never minted on Arc; attestation likely complete by now but receiveMessage was never called. Can manually mint by POSTing /api/agent/execute against it with new code. (2) AIG admin wallet has Arc gas (proven now) -- no immediate top-up needed but monitor. (3) Net amount minted (0.996383 of 1 USDC burned, ~0.36% fee) is higher than 1bps protocol minimum -- need to confirm whether CCTPv2 actual Sepolia->Arc rate is higher than the minimumFee API suggests, or whether there is an Arc-side gas/fee component. (4) Phase 03 status in roadmap_AIG.json may need updating from done_with_concerns to done. (5) Vercel deploy (Phase 04) still pending USER action; local proof now exists.

logs.title: v2 prod brought online: pushed 8 commits, fixed SSE Vercel lifetime, surfaced missing admin key
logs.what: User completed Vercel env flip + redeploy but prod still failed at bridge step. Diagnosed in sequence: (1) origin/main was 8 commits behind local — Vercel was building stale code with v1 4-arg depositForBurn against v2 7-arg TokenMessenger contract (silent revert in OKX simulation, "Third-party contract execution error"). Pushed 7b3f335..0e9cf5f. (2) After push + auto-build, prod SSE stream got cut at ~2.3s after the "bridging" event — well before receiveMessage could send the Arc mint tx (verified by Arc admin nonce staying at 1). Root cause: route handler returned Response while runPipeline ran fire-and-forget — Vercel serverless tears down the function when handler returns, killing the background pipeline. Refactored execute/route.ts to anchor pipeline in ReadableStream.start() callback so Vercel keeps function alive until controller.close(). Also added maxDuration=60, runtime=nodejs, dynamic=force-dynamic route segment config; X-Accel-Buffering: no header to disable proxy buffering. Made receiveMessage non-blocking on Arc receipt (writeContract returns tx hash, waitForTransactionReceipt fires detached) so SSE response can close in ~2s. (3) After SSE refactor, prod now correctly delivered an error event: "receiveMessage: AIG_ADMIN_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY missing or malformed". Vercel is missing the admin private key env var (or it has wrong name — user has PRIVATE_KEY and TEST_PRIVATE_KEY flagged "Needs Attention" but code reads AIG_ADMIN_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY exactly). Salvageable test burn pending: 0x061ad3cafdb7844482e59e1ded5855aa9819620abaac05fc6601fb8e1e4e399a (Iris status complete; will mint instantly once env added + redeployed). Commits e46fe15 (route config + non-blocking receipt) + 013f43f (ReadableStream refactor) pushed.
